title: Profit-maximizing behavior in perfectly competitive factor markets
description: Practice what you have learned about the way firms choose the optimal use of inputs.
topic_path: Optimal choice of factors in perfectly competitive factor markets
Profit-maximizing behavior in perfectly competitive factor markets

assessment_items.question_content:
The firm Gambol and Japes produces and sells novelty hats in an oligopoly market and hires labor in a perfectly competitive labor market.

**Which of the following statements must be true about the labor this firm hires?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The marginal revenue product of labor equals the wage rate.
The firm chooses the wages it pays for labor.
The marginal factor cost of labor is greater than the wage rate.
The marginal product of labor equals the marginal product of capital.
The marginal revenue product of labor equals the price of the good produced.

assessment_items.question_content:
Usain’s Cobalt produces cobalt packs in a perfectly competitive labor market, and the market wage is $\$20$ per unit of labor. 

The firm’s marginal product of the $200^{\text{th}}$ unit of labor is $30$ cobalt packs in an hour, and the price of a cobalt pack is $\$5$.

**What is the firm’s marginal revenue product of labor ($MRP_L$), and what should its hiring strategy be?**  

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: $MRP_L$ is $\$150$;  hire more labor.
The marginal revenue product of labor is $\$4000$, and the firm should hire more labor.
The marginal revenue product of labor is $\$20$ and the firm should hire less labor.
The marginal revenue product of labor is $\$20$ and the firm should hire more labor.
The marginal revenue product of labor is $\$5$ and the firm should hire less labor.

assessment_items.question_content:
A producer of Quidditch brooms employs $400$ units of labor at $\$10$ each, and the rental rate of capital is $\$200$. The marginal product of different units of labor used to produce Quidditch brooms are shown in this table. 

Quantity of Capital | Marginal product of capital
-|-
$10$ | $8200$
$20$ | $8000$
$30$ | $7800$
$40$ | $7600$
$50$ | $7400$

**How much capital should the firm employ to maximize profit?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
The profit-maximizing and cost minimizing choice of inputs is the quantity where the marginal product per dollar spent is equal for all inputs used.

The marginal product per dollar spent on labor is $40$ brooms:

$\begin{align}\dfrac{MP_L}{w} & = \dfrac{400}{\$10}\\\\
& = 40\end{align}$

We can figure out the marginal product per dollar spent on capital for each unit of output when capital costs \$200 per unit:

Quantity of Capital | Marginal product of capital (MP_K) | Marginal product per dollar spent
-|-|-
10 | 8200 | $\dfrac{8200}{\$200} = 41$
20 | 8000 | $\dfrac{8000}{\$200} = 40$
30 | 7800 | $\dfrac{7800}{\$200} = 39$
40 | 7600 | $\dfrac{7600}{\$200} = 38$
50 | 7400 | $\dfrac{7400}{\$200} = 37$
When the firm uses 20 units of capital, the marginal product per dollar spent on capital equals the marginal product per dollar spent on labor:

$\begin{align} \dfrac{MP_K}{r} & = \dfrac{8000}{\$200}\\\\
 & = 40\\\\
\dfrac{MP_L}{w} &= \dfrac{400}{\$10}\\\\
& = 40\end{align}$

assessment_items.question_content:
Slytherthings, Inc. is a perfectly competitive firm producing lockets. It pays $\$60$ per unit for the $10$ units of capital it uses, and the marginal product of the $10^{\text{th}}$ unit of capital is $420$ lockets. The firm can’t change its use of capital in the short run, but it can change its use of labor, and it can hire as much labor as it wants at a wage of $\$30$. The firm’s production function is given in the table shown here:

Units of labor | Total output
-|-
$0$ | $0$
$1$ | $750$
$2$ | $1420$
$3$ | $1920$
$4$ | $2270$
$5$ | $2480$
$6$ | $2580$
$7$ | $2540$

**How many units of labor should the firm hire to minimize costs and maximize profits?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

The value of a firm’s marginal product of labor $(VMPL)$ equals the marginal revenue product of labor $(MRP_L)$.

**What kind of market does this firm sell its product in?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: a perfectly competitive market
a monopolistically competitive market
a monopoly market
a monopsony market
an oligopoly market

assessment_items.question_content:
Madam Malkin’s produces and sells robes, and the wage it pays its workers is set in the labor market. The value of the firm's marginal product of labor $(MP_L)$ decreases as it hires more workers, even though it can always sell a robe for the same price.

**Which of the following is true based on the information above?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The firm sells in a perfectly competitive market and hires labor in a perfectly competitive market.
The firm sells in a perfectly competitive market and hires labor in an imperfectly competitive market.
The firm sells in an imperfectly competitive market and hires labor in a perfectly competitive market.
The firm sells in an imperfectly competitive market and hires labor in an imperfectly competitive market.
The firm sells in a perfectly competitive market, but we cannot determine the competitiveness of the labor market.

assessment_items.question_content:
Hufflepuff Industries produces badger sweaters and currently uses $20$ units of labor and $30$ units of capital. The marginal product of the $20th$ unit of labor is $100$ sweaters, and the marginal product of the $30th$ unit of capital is $200$ sweaters.

**If the cost of each unit of labor $(w)$ is $\$20$ and this firm minimizes costs, what is the rental rate of capital $(r)$?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
A firm minimizes costs when the marginal product per dollar spent on each input is equal. 
If only capital and labor are used in production, this can be represented by the relationship:

$\dfrac{MP_L}{w} = \dfrac{MP_K}{r}$
Where $MP_L$ is the marginal product of labor, $MP_K$ is the marginal product of capital, w is the cost of labor (the wage rate), and r is the cost of capital (the rental rate of capital).
We can use the values given for $MP_L$, $MP_K$, and $w$:

$\begin{align} \dfrac{MP_L}{w} &= \dfrac{MP_K}{r}\\\\
\dfrac{100}{\$20} &= \dfrac{200}{r}\end{align}$
Solving for $r$, we can find the rental rate of capital that equates the marginal product per dollar spent on each input:

$\begin{align} \dfrac{MP_L}{w} &= \dfrac{MP_K}{r}\\\\
\dfrac{100}{\$20} &= \dfrac{200}{r}\\\\
5 & = \dfrac{200}{r}\\\\
r \times 5 & = 200\\\\
r & = 200 \div 5\\\\
r & = 40\end{align}$

assessment_items.question_content:
Kraken Katchers, Inc. sells cryptid catching services in an oligopoly market. Its marginal product of capital per dollar spent on capital is $80$.

**What is the marginal product of labor if the wage rate is $\$20$ and the firm minimizes costs?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
